problems.problem: Need feedback signals to decide if we continue making AI x Creativity interviews
problems.description: The author has just finished 'Whoa, Vol II: Conversations on AI x Creativity' and explicitly asks readers to 'send us a signal so we know you want us to keep making them.' They describe the project as 'a labor of love (emphasis on labor!)' indicating it's resource-intensive and they're at a decision point about continuing.
evidence: This project has been a labor of love (emphasis on labor!)
If you've enjoyed these conversations, send us a signal so we know you want us to keep making them
problems.email_open_reasoning: The author is explicitly asking for feedback/signals to make a go/no-go decision about continuing this project. They've just completed Vol II and are clearly at an inflection point. A stranger offering concrete feedback, audience insights, or validation would be highly relevant right now.
problems.persistence_reasoning: This is a immediate, time-sensitive decision point. They've just completed Vol II and need to decide soon whether to invest in Vol III. This is a 1-2 month window before they either commit or move on.
problems.source_post: oliver burkeman is doubling down on the purely human
